name: Champs-Élysées

spec.method_detail: Shake with ice. Double strain into chilled coupe.

history: From Nina Toye and Arthur H. Adair's 'Drinks Long and Short' (1925). It's a Sidecar variant with Chartreuse replacing the orange liqueur — the herbal complexity of Chartreuse plays surprisingly well against cognac. Named for the famous Parisian boulevard.
